name: azure-aigateway
description: Configure Azure API Management (APIM) as AI Gateway to secure, observe, control AI models, MCP servers, agents. Helps with rate limiting, semantic caching, content safety, load balancing. USE FOR: AI Gateway, APIM, setup gateway, configure gateway, add gateway, model gateway, MCP server, rate limit, token limit, semantic cache, content safety, load balance, OpenAPI import, convert API to MCP. DO NOT USE FOR: deploy models (use microsoft-foundry), Azure Functions (use azure-functions), databases (use azure-postgres).
capabilities: Deploy Azure API Management as AI Gateway with Basicv2 SKU
Configure semantic caching to reduce AI costs by 60-80%
Set up token rate limiting per subscription or IP address
Implement content safety with jailbreak detection
Import OpenAPI specifications and convert APIs to MCP servers
Configure managed identity authentication for Azure AI services
author: microsoft
securitySummary: This is an official Microsoft skill for configuring Azure API Management as an AI Gateway. All static findings are false positives: external_commands are legitimate Azure CLI commands for infrastructure deployment, network findings are Microsoft documentation URLs, and 'jailbreak' references describe a content safety security feature that detects/blocks jailbreak attempts, not a vulnerability.
valueStatement: Secure and control AI models, MCP servers, and agents with Azure API Management. This skill helps set up rate limiting, semantic caching, content safety, and load balancing for AI deployments.
userTitle: Configure Azure AI Gateway with APIM
limitations: Does not deploy Azure AI models (use microsoft-foundry skill)
Does not manage Azure Functions (use azure-functions skill)
Requires Azure subscription and appropriate permissions
Basicv2 SKU has limited features compared to Premium tier

useCases.title: Secure AI Model Access
useCases.description: Add rate limiting, token quotas, and authentication to Azure OpenAI or AI Foundry deployments

useCases.title: Reduce AI Costs with Caching
useCases.description: Implement semantic caching to cache similar prompts and reduce API calls to AI services

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers integrating APIs with AI agents
useCases.title: Convert APIs to MCP Servers
useCases.description: Expose existing APIs as Model Context Protocol servers for AI agent tool discovery

promptTemplates.title: Quick Gateway Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up an Azure AI Gateway using API Management with Basicv2 SKU in resource group rg-aigateway in eastus2 region
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting started with AI Gateway deployment

promptTemplates.title: Add Rate Limiting
promptTemplates.prompt: Add token rate limiting to my AI Gateway. Limit to 500 tokens per minute per subscription
promptTemplates.scenario: Configuring token quotas for cost control

promptTemplates.title: Enable Semantic Caching
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure semantic caching on my API Management instance with 0.8 similarity threshold and 120 second TTL
promptTemplates.scenario: Reducing AI API costs with intelligent caching

promptTemplates.title: Add Content Safety
promptTemplates.prompt: Add content safety filtering to block harmful content and detect jailbreak attempts with severity threshold 4
promptTemplates.scenario: Securing AI endpoints from abusive content

bestPractices: Use Basicv2 SKU for faster deployment (5-10 min) and lower cost
Enable managed identity instead of API keys for backend authentication
Configure semantic caching with 0.7-0.8 threshold for optimal hit rates
Use azure-openai-emit-token-metric for cost tracking and monitoring
antiPatterns: Using Premium SKU when Basicv2 meets requirements (slower deployment)
Hardcoding API keys instead of using managed identity
Setting score-threshold too high (>0.9) causing no cache hits
Disabling content safety monitoring in production AI deployments

faq.question: What is Azure AI Gateway?
faq.answer: Azure AI Gateway is Azure API Management configured to secure, observe, and control AI models, MCP servers, and agents. It provides rate limiting, caching, content safety, and load balancing.

faq.question: Which APIM SKU should I use?
faq.answer: Use Basicv2 SKU for AI Gateway workloads. It supports all AI Gateway policies, deploys faster (5-10 min), and costs less than Premium.

faq.question: How does semantic caching reduce costs?
faq.answer: Semantic caching stores AI responses for similar prompts. With 0.8 similarity threshold, similar queries return cached results instead of calling the AI model, reducing costs by 60-80%.

faq.question: Can I convert my API to an MCP server?
faq.answer: Yes, Pattern 10 in this skill shows how to convert existing APIM APIs to MCP servers that AI agents can discover and use as tools.

faq.question: How does content safety work?
faq.answer: The llm-content-safety policy filters harmful content across 8 severity categories. Enable shield-prompt to detect jailbreak attempts that try to bypass AI safety controls.

faq.question: Do I need managed identity?
faq.answer: Yes, managed identity is recommended for production. It avoids storing API keys and provides secure authentication to Azure AI services like Azure OpenAI.
